channel.title: Rohan's Engineering Portfolio
channel.description: Recent content on Rohan's Engineering Portfolio

item.title: Education Details
item.description:
Detailed Education Background

Bachelor of Science in Engineering

Institution: Your University Name
Duration: 2021 - 2025
GPA: 3.8/4.0

Relevant Coursework

  • Embedded Systems: Learned microcontroller programming, sensor integration, and real-time operating systems using ESP32 and Arduino platforms.
  • Data Structures and Algorithms: Mastered fundamental data structures, algorithm analysis, and optimization techniques in C++ and Python.
  • Machine Learning: Explored supervised and unsupervised learning algorithms, neural networks, and practical applications using Python and TensorFlow.
  • Computer Networks: Studied network protocols, TCP/IP, routing, and network security fundamentals.
  • Software Engineering: Learned software development methodologies, version control (Git), testing, and agile practices.
  • Digital Signal Processing: Covered signal analysis, filtering, Fourier transforms, and their applications in engineering.

Projects and Achievements

  • Developed an IoT weather monitoring system as part of the embedded systems course
  • Implemented machine learning models for predictive analytics in data science projects
  • Contributed to open-source projects on GitHub, gaining experience in collaborative development

High School Diploma

Institution: Your High School
Duration: 2017 - 2021
GPA: 4.0/4.0

item.title: High School Academic Details
item.description:
High School Academic Details

Academic Performance

Weighted GPA (Grades 9-12): 4.50

Notable Courses Taken

Mathematics

  • Honors Precalculus: Advanced study of precalculus concepts including trigonometry, functions, and analytic geometry.
  • AP Calculus BC: Advanced Placement Calculus BC course covering differential and integral calculus, sequences, and series. Achieved a 5 on the AP exam and 5 on the AB subscore.

Computer Science

  • Computer Programming 1-2: Introduction to programming fundamentals, algorithms, and software development using languages like Java and Python.
  • AP Computer Science A (APCSA): Advanced Placement course in Java programming, object-oriented design, and algorithms. Scored a 5 on the AP exam.
  • AP Computer Science Principles (APCSP): In progress - Exploring computational thinking, programming, and the impact of computing on society.
  • Teacher Assistant for Computer Science 1-2: Served as a teaching assistant, helping peers with programming concepts and debugging.

Social Studies

  • AP World History: Comprehensive study of world history from ancient civilizations to modern times. Earned a 5 on the AP exam.
  • AP U.S. History (APUSH): In-depth exploration of American history from colonization to present. Achieved a 4 on the AP exam.

Science

  • Honors Physics: Advanced physics course covering mechanics, electricity, magnetism, and modern physics.
  • AP Chemistry: In progress - Advanced study of chemical principles, reactions, and laboratory techniques.

English

  • Honors Advanced Exposition: In progress - Advanced writing and rhetoric course focusing on expository and argumentative writing.

Other

  • Intro to Engineering Design 1-2: Introduction to engineering principles, design processes, and CAD software.
  • AP Seminar: Interdisciplinary course developing research, analysis, and presentation skills. Scored a 4 on the AP exam.
  • AP Research: In progress - Independent research project culminating in a scholarly paper and presentation.
  • AP Macro/Micro Economics: In progress - Study of macroeconomic and microeconomic principles, including fiscal and monetary policy.

Honors and Achievements

  • Redwood Honors Society: Member of the school’s honors society, recognizing academic excellence and leadership.
  • California Scholarship Federation: Active member of the statewide organization honoring academic achievement and community service.

Extracurricular Involvement

Beyond academics, I was actively involved in STEM clubs, robotics competitions, and leadership roles that complemented my coursework and developed practical skills in engineering and programming.

item.title: Project 1
item.description:
Quick Description: A weather station connected to IoT cloud, updateding cloud variables.

Description: This is my first project, where I used an ESP-32 Microcontroller to power a DHT-11 sensor and create a weather station connected to the cloud via Arduino’s IoT cloud device service. I sought to introduce myself to the connection between hardware and software, and thought that this project would be a good start

item.title: Test
item.description:
Some content

int x;
int y;
Serial.out(x+y);
